ner. It is safe to assume you know N (the total number of threads in the
running program) and that all N threads will try to enter the barrier. Again,

other integers to count things. See barrier.c for details.

1.content:
10,000 concurrent clients × 256KB = ~2.5GB just for buffers. This is why:
  - Thread-per-client scales badly (threads + memory)
  - epoll/select + thread pool works better (you have 10k listening sockets but only a few worker threads doing real
  work)

RAG Integration with Claude Code — Handoff Summary
Goal
Build a local RAG system for textbooks, exposed as an MCP tool to Claude Code.

Stack decided

Embedding: nomic-embed-text via Ollama (local, CPU-friendly)
Vector DB: LanceDB (embedded, no separate container/daemon)
Sparse search: rank-bm25 (pure Python, no server)
Search strategy: Hybrid (60% dense cosine + 40% BM25 normalized)
OCR fallback: pytesseract for scanned PDFs, marker-pdf for math-heavy
Transport: MCP over stdio (runs inside existing devcontainer, not separate container)

2.content:
Key design decisions

Runs inside existing devcontainer (DevSideQuest5), not a separate container
Textbooks bind-mounted read-only: -v /path/to/textbooks:/root/textbooks:ro
rag_db persisted as named Docker volume
OCR auto-detection: tries text layer first, falls back to tesseract if sparse
Indexing is one-time; only embedding model needs to run briefly at query time
